name: Major Scale (Ionian)
formula: W - W - H - W - W - W - H
desc: The foundation of Western harmony. All other scales and modes relate to this pattern.

name: Dorian Mode
formula: W - H - W - W - W - H - W
desc: Minor with a raised 6th. The sound of jazz, funk, and Carlos Santana.

name: Phrygian Mode
formula: H - W - W - W - H - W - W
desc: Minor with a flat 2nd. Spanish, flamenco, and metal character.

name: Lydian Mode
formula: W - W - W - H - W - W - H
desc: Major with a raised 4th. Dreamy, floating, cinematic. Steve Vai, Joe Satriani.

name: Mixolydian Mode
formula: W - W - H - W - W - H - W
desc: Major with a flat 7th. Blues-rock, jam band, and classic rock essential.

name: Aeolian Mode (Natural Minor)
formula: W - H - W - W - H - W - W
desc: The natural minor scale. Relative minor of major. Darker, moodier character.

name: Locrian Mode
formula: H - W - W - H - W - W - W
desc: The darkest mode. Diminished tonic, rarely used as key center. Theoretical importance.

name: Natural Minor
formula: W - H - W - W - H - W - W
desc: The foundational minor scale. All minor harmony derives from this pattern.

name: Harmonic Minor
formula: W - H - W - W - H - 3H - H
desc: Raised 7th creates a leading tone. Stronger pull to the tonic. Classical and neo-classical.

name: Melodic Minor (Ascending)
formula: W - H - W - W - W - W - H
desc: Raised 6th and 7th. The jazz minor scale. Foundation of altered harmony.

name: Minor Pentatonic
desc: Five notes, no wrong notes. The most-used scale in rock, blues, and pop soloing.

name: Major Pentatonic
desc: Bright and open. Country, folk, and classic rock. Same shape as minor pentatonic, different root.

name: Blues Scale (Minor)
desc: Minor pentatonic plus the blue note (b5). Adds tension, grit, and soul.

name: Major Blues Scale
desc: Major pentatonic with a chromatic passing tone. Bright blues flavor. B.B. King's signature.

name: Phrygian Dominant
formula: H - 3H - H - W - H - W - W
desc: 5th mode of harmonic minor. Middle Eastern and flamenco sound. Haunting and powerful.

name: Lydian #2
formula: 3H - H - W - H - W - W - H
desc: 2nd mode of harmonic minor. Mysterious and unsettling. Used in film scoring.

name: Super Locrian bb7
formula: H - W - H - W - W - H - 3H
desc: 7th mode of harmonic minor. Extreme dissonance. Theoretical and avant-garde.

name: Lydian Dominant
formula: W - W - W - H - W - H - W
desc: 4th mode of melodic minor. Used over dominant 7#11 chords. Bartok's scale.

name: Altered Scale
formula: H - W - H - W - W - W - W
desc: 7th mode of melodic minor. Every tension altered. The sound of modern jazz improvisation.

name: Lydian Augmented
formula: W - W - W - W - H - W - H
desc: 3rd mode of melodic minor. Major with #4 and #5. Floating, otherworldly quality.

name: Dorian b2
formula: H - W - W - W - W - H - W
desc: 2nd mode of melodic minor. Phrygian with a natural 6th. Dark but not harsh.

name: Mixolydian b6
formula: W - W - H - W - H - W - W
desc: 5th mode of melodic minor. Hindu scale. Bittersweet, exotic major quality.

name: Half-Diminished (Locrian #2)
formula: W - H - W - H - W - W - W
desc: 6th mode of melodic minor. Used over half-diminished chords in jazz ii-V-i.

name: Whole Tone
formula: W - W - W - W - W - W
desc: Six equally-spaced notes. Dreamy, floating, unresolved. Debussy's signature sound.

name: Diminished (Half-Whole)
formula: H-W-H-W-H-W-H-W
desc: Eight-note symmetric scale. Used over diminished chords. Tense and angular.

name: Diminished (Whole-Half)
formula: W-H-W-H-W-H-W-H
desc: Dominant diminished scale. Used over dominant 7b9 chords in jazz.

name: Chromatic Scale
formula: All 12 semitones
desc: All twelve notes. Not a key — a tool for chromatic exercises and passing tones.

name: Augmented Scale
formula: m3 - H - m3 - H - m3 - H
desc: Alternating minor thirds and half steps. Symmetrical, mysterious, and rare.

name: Hungarian Minor
formula: W - H - 3H - H - H - 3H - H
desc: Dramatic double-augmented sound. Eastern European folk and film music.

name: Japanese (In Sen)
formula: H - 2W - W - H - 2W
desc: Five-note scale with haunting openness. Traditional Japanese koto music and ambient.

name: Double Harmonic Major
formula: H - 3H - H - W - H - 3H - H
desc: Byzantine scale. Combines harmonic minor color with major resolution. Dramatic and cinematic.

name: Hirajoshi
formula: W - H - 2W - H - 2W
desc: Japanese pentatonic. Haunting, minimal, evocative. Used in ambient and film.

name: Neapolitan Minor
formula: H - W - W - W - H - 3H - H
desc: Natural minor with a flat 2nd plus raised 7th. Dark, Classical, operatic intensity.

name: Enigmatic Scale
formula: H - 3H - W - W - W - H - H
desc: Verdi's mysterious invention. Rarely used but hauntingly unique when applied.

name: Bebop Dominant
formula: W-W-H-W-W-H-H-H
desc: Mixolydian with a chromatic passing tone. Keeps chord tones on downbeats. Charlie Parker essential.

name: Bebop Major
formula: W-W-H-W-H-H-W-H
desc: Major scale with a chromatic passing tone between 5 and 6. Smooth, flowing jazz lines.

name: Bebop Dorian
formula: W-H-H-H-W-W-H-W
desc: Dorian with a passing tone between 3 and 4. Used in minor ii-V-i lines.
